meaning: v.涂污,污损(名誉等),把(界线,视线等)弄得模糊不清,弄污n.污点
examples: The events of that day were just a blur.
Everything is a blur when I take my glasses off.
The victim's face was blotted out by a camera blur.
Monday was a blur of getting oriented and stopping in on several less than satisfying panels.
The user interface will eventually step out of the display and blur to its surroundings.
